channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canada's Flag-Bearer Kady Dandeneau Leads Team into Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony
item.description: The Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony commenced with wheelchair basketball star Kady Dandeneau carrying the Canadian flag and lawn bowler Ryan Bester as the baton-bearer, marking Canada...

item.title: Court Resolves ADC Governorship Dispute, Recognizes Lawal as Ogun Candidate
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abeokuta has resolved a long-standing dispute within the African Democratic Congress (ADC) over the governorship candidacy for Ogun State. The court declared Otunba Jimi Adebis...

item.title: Ten Patriotism Tales by Asghar Wajahat Explore National Love
item.description: The article presents ten short stories by Asghar Wajahat that critically examine patriotism through dialogues and narratives. The tales contrast historical nationalist sacrifices with modern shallow e...

item.title: Xenophobia's Economic Impact and Call for African Unity
item.description: Award-winning stylist and entrepreneur Tracy-Lee Rosslind criticizes xenophobia and its economic consequences for South Africa in a recent interview. Rosslind, who won the Fashion Stylist of the Year ...

item.title: 10 Provinces Recognized for WPS Protection Efforts
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) recently recognized 10 provincial governments as champions of the West Philippine Sea (WPS) during the Bayanihan Para sa Kanlurang Dagat ng P...

item.title: UN Warns of Escalating Middle East Conflict Risk
item.description: United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres has issued a stark warning about the escalating conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, emphasizing that it is pushing the Middle East...

item.title: Mary Trump Criticizes Uncle Donald's Economic Mismanagement and Rally Tactics
item.description: Mary Trump, niece of President Donald Trump, harshly criticized her uncle in her latest column, accusing him of attempting to obscure his mounting failures. She specifically targeted his recent event ...

item.title: News Corp Australia warns of bot detection issues
item.description: News Corp Australia has issued a message indicating that its website employs software to manage traffic from crawler bots. The notice warns that users experiencing access issues may be detected as aut...

item.title: Delhi School Switches to Hybrid Classes Amid Protests and Security Measures
item.description: RS Junior Modern School in New Delhi has shifted to a hybrid mode of education for students from Nursery to Class 5, effective from July 24, 2026, due to ongoing protests and heightened security arran...

item.title: Trump Considers Major Strike on Iran Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump is seriously considering a major military strike against Iran, according to Axios. The decision, which could happen within days, would involve operations larger than the 2024...

item.title: The Risks of Retaliating Against U.S. Tariffs Through Oil Export Cuts
item.description: Heather Exner-Pirot argues that Ontario Premier Doug Ford's threat to cut oil exports to retaliate against Donald Trump's tariffs could backfire catastrophically. Canada's economy is deeply intertwine...

item.title: Maluti-a-Phofong Municipality Faces Salary Delays Amid Financial Mismanagement and Treasury Disputes
item.description: The Maluti-a-Phofong Local Municipality has delayed July salary payments to its staff, citing withheld equitable share funds from the National Treasury as the primary reason. This follows a letter sen...

item.title: Legal challenge over prison consultation rules in Tanzania
item.description: Lawyers representing Chadema leader Tundu Lissu have filed a petition challenging prison consultation rules, arguing they violate constitutional rights to private legal communication. The case, heard ...

item.title: Philippine Stock Market Rally Driven by Fundamentals and Foreign Investments
item.description: The article discusses the current state of the Philippine stock market, highlighting a shift in investor sentiment due to geopolitical risks. While short-term volatility is driven by fears of Middle E...

item.title: Jehan Georges Vibert: French Illustrator Known for Satirical Ecclesiastical Art
item.description: Jehan Georges Vibert (1840–1902) was a renowned French academic painter and illustrator celebrated for his witty, satirical depictions of ecclesiastical life. His work blended meticulous technical ski...

item.title: UK Government Expands No 10 North Office with Plans for Regional Staff Relocation
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham's plan to establish a regional government office in Manchester, known as No 10 North, is gaining momentum. Senior ministers and aides are set to work from this new location...

item.title: GOP Senators Criticize Trump's Pressure on Senate Leader Over Voting Bill
item.description: President Donald Trump has intensified his criticism of Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD), accusing him of failing to pass the SAVE America Act, a citizenship-proof voting bill. At a rally in G...

item.title: Mark Carney Clarifies Terms of U.S.-Canada Bridge Deal After Contradictions
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's former economic advisor, Mark Carney, admitted he could have explained the details of the Canada-U.S. bridge deal better after initial contradictions arose between his ...

item.title: Advocate Challenges Judge's Conduct During Matlala Trial
item.description: During the trial of Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala, his legal representative Advocate Annelene van den Heever confronted Judge Cassim Moosa over his dismissive body language during cross-examination. The advo...

item.title: Banks Drop InstaPay Transfer Fees as Costs Reduced
item.description: This July, major banks in the Philippines have significantly reduced or completely eliminated InstaPay transfer fees, which previously ranged up to P15 per transaction. The shift marks a pivotal chang...
